page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by a 32-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 32 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, backed by 64.0 GB of memory, which translates to a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory performance, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 384.64 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 170.56 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21489.0 and a Passmark database operations score of 46924.96, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-core performance and integer operations sit in the average range, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1865.0.

The c8gd.8xlarge offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth and local NVMe storage without requiring x86 instruction sets. However, the 2.0 GB per core memory allocation represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. Additionally, while the instance handles medium-sized LLM text generation efficiently, it exhibits weak performance in prompt processing for large 70B models, recording only 5.91 tokens/sec.

This instance is suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, software compilation, and static web serving. The combination of dedicated ARMv9 cores and fast local NVMe SSD storage supports high-throughput data processing, Redis deployments, and localized containerized workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory bandwidth, with large-block reads reaching 384.64 GB/sec, and strong multi-core capabilities, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 21489.0. While the instance delivers efficient performance for database operations and software compilation, its 2.0 GB of RAM per core and weak large-model LLM prompt processing represent notable hardware tradeoffs. The c8gd.8xlarge is suited for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, static web serving, and localized data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance with 32 Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 1900 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.8xlarge features 32 Graviton ARM64 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 1900 GB NVMe SSD for compute-heavy and database workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized platform featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARMv9 Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
1900 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier memory bandwidth with 384.64 GB/sec large-block read speeds
64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per physical core
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving
